description: Labeled MN-bill retrieval eval set. Labels assigned by human/keyword reading of bill titles+summaries, NOT by vector search (#399/#400/#380). Each query resolves to exactly one bill (or its House/Senate companion pair). Read-only against production; only bills with has_current_summary, an official_url, and membership in the current legislative session were considered.

queries.question: What's in the new social media law for kids?
queries.why_this_bill: Only 2026 bill establishing requirements for social media platforms' minor accounts + enforcement (new chapter 325M). Canonical acceptance test; distinct from the many 2025 social-media proposals.

queries.question: Is there a bill making data brokers register with the state?
queries.why_this_bill: Title is uniquely 'requiring data brokers to register with the attorney general' with an account, enforcement, and civil penalties. No companion in corpus.

queries.question: Which bill would put a special tax on the big social media companies?
queries.why_this_bill: Only bill titled 'establishing an excise tax on certain social media platform businesses' (new chapter 295) — a tax mechanism, distinct from all the consumer-protection social-media bills.

queries.question: Is there a proposal to make social media apps show a mental health warning and time-use reminders?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'requiring social media platforms to post a mental health warning label and timer notifications' (amends 325M.34). The warning-label + timer combination is distinctive.

queries.question: Did someone file a bill to punish cities for flying the wrong Minnesota state flag?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'reduction to local government aid to a county or city that uses the incorrect state flag' (new chapter 477A) — a one-of-a-kind mechanism.

queries.question: Is there a bill letting renters fix problems themselves and take it out of the rent?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'providing tenants with a right to repair violations in a residential rental unit ... permitting a tenant to make deductions from rent'. The repair-and-deduct mechanism is distinctive to this bill.

queries.question: Is there a bill about the contract terms libraries have to accept for ebooks and audiobooks?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'prohibiting certain terms in libraries' electronic book and digital audiobook license agreements or contracts'. The library ebook-license angle is distinctive.

queries.question: Is there a bill to keep certain sex offenders off social media?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'prohibiting certain sex offenders from accessing social media platforms' (amends 243.055) — a public-safety mechanism distinct from the consumer-protection social-media bills.

queries.question: What is the Minnesota Age-Appropriate Design Code Act?
queries.why_this_bill: Title verbatim names 'creating the Minnesota Age-Appropriate Design Code Act' — a distinctive named act (children's online privacy / high-privacy defaults).

queries.question: What does the reproductive health equity grant fund bill do?
queries.why_this_bill: Title verbatim 'establishing the reproductive health equity grant fund' ($30M for abortion-care access). Distinct from the separate 'sexual and reproductive health services grants' bill (HF1319).

queries.question: What is a nonopioid directive and which bill authorizes it?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'authorizing the use of nonopioid directives' (a patient directive not to be administered opioids; amends 145C.01). Distinct from the nonopioid *coverage* bills HF1806/HF1807.

queries.question: Which bill increases the criminal penalties for swatting?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'increasing criminal penalties for swatting' (amends 609.78). 'Swatting' is a distinctive verbatim term appearing in only this bill/companion.

queries.question: Which bill would put sports betting to voters as a constitutional amendment tied to horse tracks and tribes?
queries.why_this_bill: The only sports-betting bill that 'proposing an amendment to the Minnesota Constitution ... authorizing sports betting if conducted by a horse racing track or Indian Tribe' — a distinct constitutional-amendment route versus the statutory-licensing sports-betting bills.

queries.question: Is there a bill banning step therapy for insulin?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'prohibiting the use of step therapy protocol for insulin drugs' (amends 256B.0625, subd 13f). 'Step therapy' + insulin is the distinctive verbatim term.

queries.question: Which bill amends Minnesota Statutes section 121A.73 to restrict phone use in schools?
queries.why_this_bill: The dedicated bill 'restricting cell phone use in schools; amending ... section 121A.73'. (A broad K-12 omnibus, SF3870, also touches 121A.73, but this is the single-purpose cell-phone bill the question describes.)

queries.question: Which bill amends section 504B.245 to stop landlords from using rent-setting algorithms trained on competitor data?
queries.why_this_bill: Only bill amending 504B.245 for this purpose: 'prohibiting the use of tenant screening software that uses nonpublic competitor data to set rent' (algorithmic rent price-fixing).

queries.question: is ther a bill to ban led amunition for huntin?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'prohibiting the use of lead ammunition when hunting; prohibiting ... lead tackle; establishing a nontoxic ammunition performance standard'. Distinctive lead-ammo/tackle ban.

queries.question: wut bill makes companies list whats in tampons and menstral products?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'requiring ingredient disclosure for menstrual products' (new chapter 325E). Distinct from the 2025 bill banning arsenic/chemicals in menstrual products (HF2983).

queries.question: is there money in a bill for the world juniors hockey tournment?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'appropriating money for the World Junior Hockey Championships' ($5M grant to Explore Minnesota). A one-of-a-kind, highly specific appropriation.

queries.question: whats the bill that funds the helmets to hardhats program?
queries.why_this_bill: Uniquely 'appropriating money for the Minnesota Helmets to Hardhats program' (grant to Building Strong Communities). 'Helmets to Hardhats' is a distinctive named program.
